entities.name: Moonshot attempt
entities.context: High-risk objective that forces skill acquisition and perspective shift beyond existing capacity

entities.name: Slam dunk success
entities.context: Safe win that operates within existing capacity, providing validation but minimal growth

entities.name: Growth dividends
entities.context: Benefits from ambitious attempts that outweigh cumulative value of safe successes

entities.name: Adaptive stress
entities.context: Mechanism similar to biological evolution through which skills and capabilities develop

entities.name: High-order skill acquisition
entities.context: Primary metric for evaluating career paths and business initiatives alongside financial ROI

entities.name: Failure with growth
entities.context: Outcome valued higher than success with stagnation in strategic planning

entities.name: Success with stagnation
entities.context: Outcome of safe wins that should be valued lower than growth-producing failures

relationships.context: Even failed moonshots produce growth dividends that outweigh slam dunk successes
High-risk objectives force structural stress that drives capability development
Stress of ambitious attempts creates new capacities beyond existing baseline
Safe wins provide validation but minimal growth, leading to stagnation
Moonshot failures leave agent at higher baseline than slam dunk successes
Skill acquisition is the mechanism through which growth dividends are realized
Strategic planning should value the former higher than the latter
